what_it_does: This file is a ledger entry documenting the NVIDIA Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-AWQ model: its metadata, download status, architecture, intended high-throughput chat role, operational unknowns, and links to related models and runtimes.
observations: Model is downloaded but has never been test-loaded.
It is an AWQ-quantized MoE with 30B total parameters and ~3B active per token, using hybrid Mamba-attention.
It is proposed as a high-throughput chat candidate via vLLM AWQ, competing with a Qwen GGUF/Ollama alternative.
Fit on 16 GB VRAM is unverified; the 17 GB on-disk size leaves a tight KV-cache margin.
The 'known-good launch config' is explicitly provisional and untested.
It requires a Nemotron-specific vLLM tool-call parser and chat template, distinct from the Qwen stack.
It cannot co-exist with the current default chat and must be deployed via a swap-only procedure.
The entry notes behavioural differences from Qwen: knowledge cutoff, post-training, and tool format.
standouts: The entry is almost entirely a hypothesis: downloaded but operationally unverified.
The AWQ-on-vLLM MoE path is chosen specifically for batched concurrency versus the GGUF/Ollama route.
VRAM fit is borderline, making load success the central open question.
conventions: Follows the standard model ledger frontmatter with type, id, filename, status, runtime, date_added, and tags.
Uses wikilink-style cross-references to related models and operations pages.
Organized into at-a-glance table, source rationale, fits-these-needs, tested performance, launch config, quirks, open questions, and connects-to sections.
Status 'downloaded' and runtime 'not-yet-served' are consistent with the content.
concerns: The 'known-good launch config' section is explicitly provisional and untested.
Many operational claims rest on unverified assumptions (VRAM fit, vLLM 0.21 support, exact tool parser name).
The date_added value of 2026-05-26 is future-dated relative to typical knowledge cutoffs.
notes: A speculative vLLM-served AWQ MoE candidate that is downloaded but not yet loaded, with tight VRAM and unresolved template/parser requirements.
questions: What is the exact vLLM tool-call parser flag for Nemotron?
Does this checkpoint have a thinking/non-thinking variant?
What is the actual VRAM load and concurrent throughput on the target 16 GB hardware?
What chat template does the checkpoint ship with, and does it need a no-think patch?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (document a candidate throughput-MoE model and its open operational questions) matches the content; it is a planning/tracking entry rather than a verified result.
apparent_themes: Operational verification before declaring a model production-ready.
Throughput-oriented model selection favoring batched vLLM over GGUF/Ollama.
Runtime and quantization path matter as much as model size.
Template and tool-format compatibility as first-class deployment constraints.
intention_signals: The file is trying to record a bet: that an AWQ-quantized Nemotron-3 MoE served via vLLM can outperform the GGUF/Ollama Qwen MoE alternative for high-throughput chat, while flagging every assumption that still needs testing.
novelty: Hybrid Mamba-attention Nemotron-3 Nano architecture combined with AWQ quantization and vLLM batched-serving candidacy, distinct from the Qwen-based default stack.
contribution: Captures a specific model candidate, its metadata, the rationale for choosing the vLLM+AWQ route, and a checklist of operational unknowns (VRAM fit, parser, chat template, throughput) so a fused system can evaluate it against alternatives.
summary_for_embedding: Ledger entry for the downloaded but not-yet-served AWQ-quantized NVIDIA Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-AWQ MoE model, documenting its candidacy for high-throughput vLLM chat and its unverified operational requirements.
intention_for_embedding: Track a potential high-throughput chat model alternative and enumerate the tests needed to validate its vLLM deployment.
